title: Boat Rental in Guadeloupe: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Guadeloupe offers some of the best sailing in the Caribbean, with steady trade winds and calm waters inside the lagoon. Renting a boat from Marina Bas-du-Fort in Pointe-à-Pitre typically costs from EUR 180 for a half-day motorboat to EUR 1,200 for a crewed catamaran. Compare verified options with a yacht charter Guadeloupe on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat rental.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

Boat rental prices in Guadeloupe range from EUR 80 per person for a shared tour to EUR 1,500 per day for a crewed luxury yacht. The exact cost depends on boat size, season, and whether a skipper is included. Below is a typical price band breakdown. You can yacht charter Guadeloupe for your chosen dates.

  • Shared group tour: from EUR 80 per person for a half-day excursion to Pigeon Island.
  • Small motorboat (5, 6 pax): from EUR 180 for a half-day without skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day (10, 12 pax): from EUR 700 with skipper and fuel.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (8, 10 pax): from EUR 1,200 per day including crew and insurance.

To find the best deal for your group size, browse owners direct on BoatHire24. You can filter by boat type, capacity, and price in EUR.

Where to set off in Guadeloupe

Most rentals depart from the main island of Grande-Terre or Basse-Terre. The busiest hub is Marina Bas-du-Fort in Pointe-à-Pitre, with 1,000 berths and full services. Other marinas include Marina de Rivière Sens in Gourbeyre, Port de Plaisance in Saint-François, and the smaller Marina de la Désirade. Many skippers also offer pick-ups from the beaches of Sainte-Anne or Le Gosier. From Saint-François, it is 12 nautical miles to Marie-Galante, a popular day trip.

Which boat to rent

Choose your boat based on group size and desired comfort. Here are the most popular categories for Guadeloupe.

  1. Dinghy or small open motorboat, best for couples or small groups exploring shallow coves; holds up to 5 guests; from EUR 180 half-day.
  2. Catamaran or monohull sailing yacht, ideal for a full-day family cruise with snorkelling stops; 8, 10 guests; from EUR 600 full-day.
  3. Motor yacht, e.g. Prestige 500, a comfortable flybridge cruiser perfect for a week-long charter; 8 guests; from EUR 950 per day. For detailed specs, see the Prestige 500 listing on BoatHire24.
  4. Luxury crewed yacht, e.g. Azimut 50, for a premium holiday with captain and host; 8, 10 guests; from EUR 1,200 per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

In Guadeloupe, a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent is generally required for bareboat charters, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. Some rental companies accept a national boating licence if accompanied by a logbook or proof of experience. For small open boats under 15 hp, no licence is often needed. Always check with the owner on BoatHire24 before booking.

In Guadeloupe, the Christmas winds blow from December to February, making the sheltered lee of Basse-Terre the best bet for smoother sailing in high season.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 1,500 per person or per boat
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence required: Usually ICC for bareboat; check with host

Questions about boat rental in Guadeloupe

What documents do I need to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

For bareboat charters you typically need a valid ICC or RYA Day Skipper, plus a credit card for the deposit; a passport is required to verify your identity.

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Guadeloupe?

Yes, you can rent a boat without a skipper if you hold the required licence, but many companies recommend a skipper for navigating the reef passages around the Grand Cul-de-Sac Marin.

What is the best season for sailing in Guadeloupe?

The best season is from December to April, when the trade winds blow steadily at 15, 20 knots and the seas are calm inside the lagoon.

Are there child-friendly boat rentals in Guadeloupe?

Many catamaran and motorboat rentals provide life jackets and allow children aged 2 and above, but you should confirm safety equipment with the owner before booking.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Guadeloupe and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
